mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Vultr's ecosystem and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Vultr CLI, which is necessary for managing your Vultr resources from the command line.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xvf vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> with your actual cluster ID.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional configurations such as autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler.
> Deploy monitoring tools.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID>

```

> Replace <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer delete --all

vultr-cli volume delete --all

```

> Delete all load balancers.
> Delete all volumes.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step upgrades the Kubernetes version of your cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> --version '1.30'

kubectl drain <NODE_NAME> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl uncordon <NODE_NAME>

```

> Replace <YOUR_CLUSTER_ID> with your actual cluster ID.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running kubectl commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or your credentials are invalid.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command with the correct cluster ID.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found' when checking nodes.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not properly created or kubeconfig is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster creation command and check your kubeconfig file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity' when creating a cluster.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the selected region.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different region or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer not responding.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the service type is not set correctly or the load balancer is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure it is of type LoadBalancer.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr regions and improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older API versions for cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and your Vultr account is set up.
